2606.07698.title: Pharmacogenomic Knowledge Graph Augmentation for Graph Neural Network-Based Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction
2606.07698.paper_summary: Graph neural networks (GNNs) applied to drug-drug interaction (DDI) prediction rely exclusively on molecular structure encoded as SMILES-derived graphs. Prior work in this series demonstrated that model performance is bounded by the structural information content of training labels -- an Information Ceiling -- that architectural refinements alone cannot overcome. The present study investigates whether pharmacogenomic prior knowledge from the PharmGKB database partially closes this ceiling by providing metabolic pathway context that is independent of, and complementary to, molecular structure. Cytochrome P450 (CYP) enzyme substrate, inhibitor, and inducer annotations for four clinically relevant isoforms (CYP2D6, CYP3A4, CYP2C19, CYP2C9) are extracted and incorporated as a 12-dimensional feature vector concatenated to the molecular embedding prior to interaction prediction. Experiments are conducted under both pair-level and drug-level data splits to quantify generalization to unseen drugs. Results indicate that knowledge graph (KG) augmentation substantially improves DDI type classification under pair-level split conditions (F1-macro: 0.532 vs. 0.241 baseline), while binary interaction detection and drug-level generalization remain bounded by the Information Ceiling (AUC inflation: 0.224 vs. 0.250 baseline). Mechanistic validation on strictly held-out compounds confirms that augmentation preferentially improves CYP2C9-mediated interaction prediction, with probabilities increasing from 0.033-0.117 (baseline) to 0.560-0.586 (KG-augmented). An extension to single-molecule toxicity prediction on the Tox21 benchmark confirms that the effect is contingent on pharmacogenomic annotation coverage. These findings motivate the multimodal framework proposed for the subsequent study in this series.
2606.07698.paper_summary_zh: 圖神經網絡（GNNs）應用於藥物-藥物相互作用（DDI）預測完全依賴於作為SMILES衍生圖編碼的分子結構。先前的研究顯示，模型性能受到訓練標籤的結構信息內容的限制——即信息天花板——而僅僅通過架構改進無法克服。當前研究探討了來自PharmGKB數據庫的藥物基因組學先驗知識是否部分關閉這一天花板，通過提供獨立於分子結構且互補的代謝途徑上下文。提取四種臨床相關亞型（CYP2D6、CYP3A4、CYP2C19、CYP2C9）的細胞色素P450（CYP）酶底物、抑制劑和誘導劑註釋，並將其作為12維特徵向量，在進行相互作用預測之前與分子嵌入進行串接。實驗在配對級別和藥物級別數據拆分下進行，以量化對未見藥物的泛化能力。結果表明，知識圖（KG）增強在配對級別拆分條件下顯著改善DDI類型分類（F1-macro: 0.532對比0.241基線），而二元相互作用檢測和藥物級別泛化仍然受到信息天花板的限制（AUC膨脹: 0.224對比0.250基線）。對嚴格保留的化合物進行的機制驗證確認，增強優先改善CYP2C9介導的相互作用預測，概率從0.033-0.117（基線）增至0.560-0.586（KG增強）。對Tox21基準的單分子毒性預測的擴展確認，這一效果依賴於藥物基因組學註釋的覆蓋範圍。這些發現激勵了為本系列後續研究提出的多模態框架。
2606.07698.author: Juergen Dietrich et.al.
